title: Navigation History, Immutable Snapshots, Back, and Bounded Undo · Type-System Trace: Pokedex Ts Back User Contract
summary: History stores mutable references, pushes attempts before success, back triggers new hidden network work, unlimited pages accumulate, and undo can resurrect a stale pending request. Predict the user decision, runtime value, strict type claim, promise state, API boundary, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex ts back user contract increment in bounded immutable navigation timeline. Learners progress through prediction,…
objectives: Define back as prior admitted view, not arithmetic or browser history, and expose when no snapshot exists.
Script rapid next, back, abort, failure, short page, repeated view, and out-of-order responses and assert every state.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
